field.value: The iconographic model for the goddess Aphrodite (Venus) stepping out of the bath is the image venerated in the temple dedicated to the goddess on the Greek island of Cnidos. The image of the nude female figure seeking to cover herself in a modest gesture was very popular, and was widely circulated in both Greek and Roman periods. Many copies exist of the same original, which was the work of Praxiteles, a Greek sculptor of the fourth century BCE. The copy was restored by Ippolito Bazzi, who completed a female torso with restored head, arms, and legs, on the side he placed the jug covered by a drapery held in the figure's left hand.
